title: Allergy list update

investigations.text: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.
investigations.rationale: The exact structured-documentation section supports recording a suspected or confirmed drug name, reaction signs and symptoms, severity, date, timing, indication, doses before onset, route, status, and specialist results without confirming allergy.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.9 — recording and sharing drug-allergy information

medication.text: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
medication.rationale: The exact medicines-communication section supports clinician-entered reconciliation, communication, patient questions, safety-net, and follow-up documentation without changing the allergy list automatically.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

source_limitations: Specific substance, reaction, date, severity, route, confirmation status, specialist result, non-drug allergy, list action, and communication remain unsupported unless entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
